stories.title: Sinner wins Rome — Career Golden Masters complete

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Jannik Sinner wins Rome 6-4, 6-4. Career Golden Masters COMPLETE.

Only the 2nd man ever — after Djokovic — to win all 9 Masters 1000 titles. First Italian champion in Rome since Panatta in 1976.

#TennisRecap #ItalianOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
34 consecutive M1000 wins. 6 titles in a row. MC + Madrid + Rome in the same season.

Only Nadal (2010) swept all 3 clay M1000s in a single year before Sinner.

Roland Garros is next.

#Sinner #ItalianOpen #ATPTennis

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

History was made at the Foro Italico on Sunday.

Jannik Sinner defeated Casper Ruud 6-4, 6-4 in the Italian Open final, completing the Career Golden Masters — winning all nine Masters 1000 titles. He is only the second man in history to do it, after Novak Djokovic.

The victory also ended a 50-year wait for an Italian champion in Rome. The last? Adriano Panatta in 1976.

Sinner is now on a 34-match Masters 1000 winning streak and has won six consecutive titles. He also became only the second player after Rafael Nadal (2010) to sweep all three clay-court Masters 1000 events — Monte Carlo, Madrid, and Rome — in the same season.

"There's no better place to complete this set," Sinner said after the match.

Ruud continues to be the unluckiest finalist in tennis — now 0-5 in Rome finals and 0-6 in Masters 1000 finals overall.

The French Open is next. What do you think happens when Sinner gets to Roland Garros?

---

article.headline: Sinner Wins Rome, Completes Career Golden Masters: Only the Second Man in History

stories.title: Roland Garros qualifying opens — Dimitrov, Goffin, Andreescu headline

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Roland Garros qualifying is underway.

Dimitrov. Goffin. Andreescu. Pliskova. Stephens. Badosa. All fighting for a main draw spot.

Draw ceremony: May 21. Main draw: May 25.

#RolandGarros #FrenchOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Gael Monfils plays his FINAL Roland Garros this week.

On Thursday at Philippe-Chatrier — one last time in Paris for one of tennis's great entertainers.

#Monfils #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:30 AM ET

The road to Roland Garros officially begins today.

Qualifying kicked off this morning at the Stade Roland-Garros, with 128 men and 128 women competing for 16 spots in each main draw. The action runs through Friday, with the main draw ceremony on Thursday May 21.

Some of the biggest names in the qualifying field this year:

Men: Grigor Dimitrov, David Goffin (in his farewell Roland Garros season)
Women: Bianca Andreescu, Karolina Pliskova, Sloane Stephens, Paula Badosa

And on Thursday evening, the Philippe-Chatrier court hosts a special farewell: Gaël Monfils plays his final Roland Garros before retiring at the end of 2026.

The main draw begins Sunday May 25. Jannik Sinner is the overwhelming favorite. Alcaraz is out with a wrist injury. Djokovic's participation remains uncertain.

Who are you most excited to watch at Roland Garros this year?

---

article.headline: Roland Garros Qualifying Opens: Dimitrov, Goffin, Andreescu Fight for Main Draw Spots

stories.title: ATP rankings after Rome — Darderi career-high No. 16, Ruud back in top 20

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Post-Rome ATP rankings:

Luciano Darderi: career-high No. 16 (first M1000 SF)
Casper Ruud: back in the top 20
Daniil Medvedev: up 2 spots after SF

Roland Garros draw on Thursday.

#ATPTennis #RolandGarros

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:30 PM ET

The Rome dust has settled — here's how the ATP rankings look heading into Roland Garros week.

The biggest mover: Luciano Darderi climbs to a career-high No. 16 after reaching his first Masters 1000 semifinal on home clay at the Foro Italico. The Argentine-born Italian made his country proud in Rome.

Casper Ruud returns to the top 20 after his run to the final. He arrived at the tournament unranked in the top 20 and leaves with points in the bank.

Daniil Medvedev also picks up two places after reaching the semifinal.

The seedings for Roland Garros will be determined Thursday at the draw ceremony. With Sinner at No. 1, Alcaraz absent, and Djokovic uncertain, the men's draw could look dramatically different from recent Grand Slams.

Which player are you most excited to follow at Roland Garros?

---

article.headline: ATP Rankings After Rome: Darderi Hits Career-High No. 16, Ruud Returns to Top 20

stories.title: WTA rankings shake-up — Svitolina rises, Cirstea cracks top 20 for first time

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Sorana Cirstea — 36 years old, final season — earns her FIRST career top-20 ranking after her Rome run.

She beat world No. 1 Sabalenka along the way.

Tennis never stops delivering.

#WTATennis #TennisRecap

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM ET

The WTA rankings after Rome are a reminder that this sport always finds a way to surprise you.

Elina Svitolina rises to No. 7 after winning her third Rome title — beating world Nos. 2, 3, and 4 inside four days. She arrives at Roland Garros as the form player on the women's tour.

Sorana Cirstea — 36 years old and playing in what she has called her final season — earns a first-ever top-20 ranking. She reached the Rome semifinal after defeating world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka. A career high at 36.

Jasmine Paolini, the defending Rome champion, drops outside the top 10 after a tough title defense.

Zheng Qinwen falls 21 places after a difficult week.

Roland Garros is shaping up to be one of the most open women's tournaments in years. Who do you think wins the Coupe Suzanne-Lenglen?

---

article.headline: WTA Rankings After Rome: Cirstea Makes Top-20 Debut at 36, Svitolina Surges to No. 7

stories.title: Sinner heads to Roland Garros — can he complete a Grand Slam Golden Set?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Career Golden Masters: done.
Six straight M1000 titles: done.
34-match M1000 win streak: running.

The only thing left on Sinner's list? Roland Garros. Draw is Thursday.

#Sinner #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen

facebook.long_form:
Window: 6:00 PM ET

Jannik Sinner is heading to Roland Garros with a target list that has only one item left on it.

After completing the Career Golden Masters in Rome — winning all nine Masters 1000 titles — Sinner now has every tournament in the game except the French Open. His best result there is a quarterfinal in 2020.

He arrives in Paris on a 34-match Masters 1000 winning streak with six consecutive titles. Carlos Alcaraz is out with a wrist injury. Novak Djokovic is uncertain due to a shoulder problem. No defending champion from either side has a tougher path than Sinner.

The draw ceremony is Thursday May 21. The main draw begins Sunday May 25.

The question is no longer whether Sinner can win Roland Garros. The question is who can stop him.

Who do you see as Sinner's biggest threat in Paris?

---

article.headline: Sinner Heads to Roland Garros: The Only Grand Slam He Has Never Won
